user: How does QLoRA work for fine-tuning?
assistant: QLoRA (Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation) is a highly efficient fine-tuning technique that reduces memory usage significantly. It works by quantizing a large language model to 4-bit precision using NormalFloat4 (NF4) while freezing its original weights. Then, small, trainable low-rank adapter matrices are added to specific layers of the model. This allows you to fine-tune massive models like Mistral on a single consumer GPU without compromising performance.
